message:
feat(server): ATC reservation event log, TUI F1 help binding, system_health panel polish

Server — ATC reservation event log
----------------------------------
`AtcEngine` grows a bounded `VecDeque<ReservationEvent>` ring buffer
(cap `MAX_RESERVATION_EVENT_LOG = 512`) that records granted /
released / expired reservation lifecycle events for later
correlation with the expiry sweep (tracking bead `br-bn0vb.10`).
Each entry carries the event kind, agent name, project, paths, and
a microsecond timestamp. The log lives next to the existing
`agent_intervention_counts` and `prev_snapshot_state` fields so one
mutex guard sees the full post-tick ATC state.

Server — dispatch + polling wiring
----------------------------------
`crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs` grows ~800 lines of new
dispatch plumbing tying the ATC engine and the new DB surface
together: the labeled-counter registrations from the previous
commit are emitted at every producer site, reservation grant /
release / expire sites push into the ATC event log, and the
canonical rollup tick path (`refresh_atc_rollups_on_operator_tick`
introduced in `br-bn0vb.4`) now also consumes from the new DB
queries landed in `feat(db): ATC v17 schema family ...`.

TUI — help-binding migration from `?` to `F1`
---------------------------------------------
`crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_keymap.rs` moves the
"toggle help" global binding from `?` to `F1` across the vim,
emacs, and minimal profiles. `F1` is an escape-code binding — it
is not `text_suppressible`, so it keeps working when a text input
has focus (where `?` was previously suppressed and therefore
silently unavailable). `action_id_for_label` accepts both `F1` and
legacy `?` for back-compat so existing muscle memory still fires
the help overlay. `label_to_keycodes("F1")` → `vec![KeyCode::F(1)]`
is added next to the existing F12 mapping.

TUI screens — System Health and supporting polish
-------------------------------------------------
- `tui_screens/system_health.rs` (+158 lines) gets the ATC
  subsystem integrated into the existing health grid: kill-switch
  state, resolution-sweep p95, derivation-error rate, and the new
  reservation-event-log depth are surfaced as live rows that mirror
  the Prometheus alerts in `docs/ATC_ALERTS_EXAMPLE.yaml`. Panels
  reuse the same `LabeledCounterMap::snapshot()` surface the
  Prometheus scraper is fed from, so TUI operators see exactly the
  same numbers a paging page would see.
- `tui_screens/mod.rs` picks up the ATC screen wiring (the "16th
  screen" documented in the earlier README/AGENTS.md update).
- `tui_screens/agents.rs`, `tui_screens/dashboard.rs`,
  `tui_screens/threads.rs` get small per-panel updates that the
  dispatch rewire required (passing the engine handle through) plus
  cargo-fmt wrapping on long `row.get(N).and_then(...)` chains.
- `tui_app.rs`, `tui_chrome.rs`, `tui_poller.rs` pick up the
  corresponding plumbing updates at the top of the TUI loop so the
  new screen participates in the poll cycle and the chrome frame
  counts the screen correctly.

Tools — resources.rs test cosmetics
-----------------------------------
`crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/resources.rs` picks up a
cargo-fmt pass over the `resource_shape_tests` module: long
`agents_array.iter().any(...)` chains and
`conn.query_sync("…", &[])` calls get compressed back onto a
single line where the formatter preferred that over
multi-line layout. No behavioural change — the assertions are
identical.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
